sourceTitle: Mastering the RACI model for effective project management

questions.question: In the RACI model, what does the "R" stand for?

options.text: Responsible
options.explanation: Correct – "R" designates the person who actually does the work.

options.text: Reviewed
options.explanation: Wrong – "Reviewed" is not part of the RACI acronym.

options.text: Reporter
options.explanation: Wrong – "Reporter" is not a RACI role.

options.text: Resource
options.explanation: Wrong – "Resource" is not used in RACI.

questions.question: Which Notion feature helps you quickly create repetitive task pages with the same structure?

options.text: Template button
options.explanation: Correct – the template button lets you define reusable content for new pages.

options.text: Relation property
options.explanation: Incorrect – relations link pages but don’t replicate content.

options.text: Inline equation
options.explanation: Incorrect – equations are for math, not page duplication.

options.text: Rollup property
options.explanation: Incorrect – rollups aggregate data, they don’t create new pages.

questions.question: How can you set up a Notion view that automatically shows only the tasks assigned to the user who is currently signed in?

options.text: Create a view filtered by the "Me" property
options.explanation: Correct – the "Me" filter dynamically matches the logged‑in user.

options.text: Manually add the user’s name to a filter each time
options.explanation: Wrong – this defeats the purpose of a dynamic view.

options.text: Use a formula that references the browser’s URL
options.explanation: Incorrect – Notion formulas cannot read the URL.

options.text: Share a separate database for each team member
options.explanation: Incorrect – sharing separate databases is unnecessary and inefficient.

questions.question: Why is it advantageous to keep most of the work inside the task pages of a Notion RACI database?

options.text: Team members become familiar with all roles and can see updates in one place
options.explanation: Correct – centralizing work promotes role awareness and reduces context switching.

options.text: It automatically syncs the database with external project‑management tools
options.explanation: Wrong – Notion does not auto‑sync with external tools without integrations.

options.text: It eliminates the need for any communication channels like Slack
options.explanation: Incorrect – communication channels can still be useful for real‑time alerts.

options.text: It makes the database read‑only for all users
options.explanation: Incorrect – keeping work inside pages does not change permission levels.

questions.question: When a task requires input from several experts before it can be completed, which RACI role should those experts be assigned?

options.text: Consulted
options.explanation: Correct – "Consulted" are the people whose expertise is sought before decisions are made.

options.text: Responsible
options.explanation: Wrong – "Responsible" actually performs the work, not just provides input.

options.text: Informed
options.explanation: Incorrect – "Informed" only receives updates after decisions are made.

options.text: Accountable
options.explanation: Incorrect – there should be only one accountable person per task; experts are not typically accountable.

questions.question: What benefit does linking a Notion task page to a Slack channel provide?

options.text: Updates to the task are automatically posted to the channel, keeping the team informed
options.explanation: Correct – the integration pushes changes to Slack, enhancing visibility.

options.text: It moves the task out of Notion and into Slack permanently
options.explanation: Wrong – the link only shares updates; the task remains in Notion.

options.text: It prevents anyone from editing the task after it’s linked
options.explanation: Incorrect – linking does not affect edit permissions.

options.text: It automatically creates a new Slack workspace for the project
options.explanation: Incorrect – Slack workspaces must be created manually.

questions.question: Why should each RACI role be added as a separate property in a Notion database?

options.text: It enables you to assign and filter specific people for each role on a per‑task basis
options.explanation: Correct – separate properties make role assignment clear and allow view filtering.

options.text: It automatically generates a Gantt chart for the project
options.explanation: Wrong – role properties alone don’t create timeline visualizations.

options.text: It locks the database so that only managers can edit tasks
options.explanation: Incorrect – properties don’t control edit rights.

options.text: It merges all team members into a single dropdown list
options.explanation: Incorrect – each role remains distinct, not merged.

questions.question: A project manager assigns two people as "Accountable" for the same task. Which of the following issues is most likely to arise?

options.text: Decision‑making may become unclear because accountability is meant to be singular per task
options.explanation: Correct – RACI prescribes only one accountable person to avoid confusion over final approval.

options.text: The task will automatically be marked as complete when either person finishes their work
options.explanation: Wrong – completion status isn’t tied to the number of accountable owners.

options.text: Both people will receive duplicate Slack notifications, causing unnecessary noise
options.explanation: Incorrect – while notifications may increase, the core problem is ambiguous authority, not message volume.

options.text: Notion will prevent the task from being saved due to a validation error
options.explanation: Incorrect – Notion does not enforce a single accountable entry automatically.

questions.question: Using the "Me" filter view in Notion tailors task lists to the current user. Which limitation should a team be aware of when relying solely on this feature?

options.text: If a user is not assigned to any role on a task, the task will never appear in their view, even if they need to be informed
options.explanation: Correct – the "Me" filter only shows tasks where the user is explicitly listed in a property; they won’t see tasks where they are only "Informed" unless that property is set.

options.text: The view updates only once per day, so recent changes may be missed
options.explanation: Wrong – Notion updates filtered views in real time.

options.text: It automatically changes the user's role to "Responsible" for all displayed tasks
options.explanation: Incorrect – the filter is visual only; it does not alter role assignments.

options.text: The "Me" filter can only be applied to databases that have exactly four properties
options.explanation: Incorrect – there is no such restriction; any database with a user property can use the filter.
